title: rich glassy dutch chicken post-workout stew
description: this rich and comforting post-workout stew is a dutch-inspired recipe featuring chicken stock and a nut-free deglaze, perfect for a muscular recovery. the glassy-jelly texture comes from the slow-cooked gelatinous meat and vegetables.
instructions: heat butter in a large dutch oven over medium-high heat.
add chicken breast and cook until browned, about 5 minutes. remove from pot and set aside.
add onion, carrots, and celery to pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
add garlic to pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
sprinkle flour over vegetables and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
gradually stir in chicken stock and white wine, scraping up browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
return chicken breast to pot and simmer, uncovered, for 20 minutes, or until the meat is tender and the sauce has thickened.
stir in dijon mustard and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
season with salt and pepper to taste.
serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
